notes.note: Before going into exile Cicero had been offered a post as a legatus (a senatorial deputy serving abroad), which would have given him a lawful pretext to leave Rome and immunity from prosecution. He now regrets declining it.
C. Calpurnius Piso Frugi, the husband of Cicero's daughter Tullia, who worked tirelessly for his father-in-law's recall during the exile. He died in 57 BC before Cicero's return.
The Kalends were the first of the month; "the day before the Kalends of December" is 30 November 58 BC, dating this letter one day after Fam. 14.1.
